components.name: Windows Search Index
components.description: Removes the search indexer feature and disables the Windows Search service. Search still works, but by scanning rather than by index.
breaks: Start menu search no longer finds documents or settings by content, only apps by name
File Explorer search falls back to a live directory scan and becomes very slow on large folders
Outlook classic reports that search is unavailable and offers no results at all
Any app using the Windows Search API — including several file managers — returns empty results

components.name: System Restore
components.description: Disables System Restore and its scheduled checkpoint creation by policy.
breaks: No restore points are created, so a bad driver or update cannot be rolled back from WinRE
The System Protection tab in System Properties is present but does nothing
Some installers create a restore point as a safety step and will log a failure instead

components.name: Reserved Storage
components.description: Turns off the ~7 GB reserve Windows sets aside for updates and temporary files.
breaks: Feature updates can fail partway through on a nearly full disk instead of being blocked cleanly up front
Windows will not automatically free space for an update, so you have to manage disk space yourself

components.name: Automatic Device Encryption
components.description: Stops Windows silently BitLocker-encrypting the system drive during setup on capable hardware.
breaks: The system drive is left unencrypted, so anyone who can boot from USB can read it
You can still turn BitLocker on by hand afterwards; this only stops it happening without being asked

components.name: Windows Defender
components.description: Removes Windows Defender entirely. Core preset only, and gated behind a typed confirmation. Defender is not a removable package on this build family, so this deletes its payload and disables its services instead.
breaks: No antivirus, no real-time protection and no SmartScreen — install a replacement before going online
Tamper protection and Windows Security are gone; the Security app shows empty or errors
This CANNOT be undone on the resulting image — Defender cannot be reinstalled after the fact
Some enterprise management and attack-surface-reduction policies stop applying silently

actions.path: Program Files\Windows Defender Advanced Threat Protection

components.name: Windows Recovery Environment
components.description: Deletes WinRE, the recovery image that backs Reset This PC, Startup Repair and the advanced boot options.
breaks: Reset This PC, Startup Repair and System Image Recovery are all gone — a machine that fails to boot must be reinstalled from media
The advanced startup options menu no longer appears after failed boots
BitLocker recovery from the boot menu is unavailable, so keep the recovery key somewhere else
Cannot be restored on the resulting image without a full reinstall

components.name: Windows Update Stack
components.description: Disables the update services and orchestrator so the image never contacts Windows Update.
breaks: No security updates, ever — this image will never be patched again
Driver updates through Windows Update stop, so new hardware may have no driver at all
Store app updates that route through the update stack also stop
Turning this back on afterwards is unreliable; the services can be re-enabled but the orchestrator state is already gone

components.name: Component Store (WinSxS)
components.description: Deletes the side-by-side component store. This is the single largest saving available and the single most destructive action in the catalog.
breaks: No Windows feature or capability can ever be added, because the payload they install from is gone
sfc /scannow and DISM /RestoreHealth cannot repair anything and will fail outright
Cumulative updates cannot apply — this image is frozen at the build it was made from
Some applications that request a Windows feature at install time will fail rather than prompt
Cannot be undone: there is no way to rebuild the store on the resulting image

components.name: Servicing Stack
components.description: Deletes the servicing stack itself. Only coherent once the component store is already gone.
breaks: DISM and the package manager stop working entirely on the resulting image
Optional features cannot be listed, let alone enabled or disabled
The image cannot be serviced offline either — a future TinyWin run against it will fail at the apply stage
Cannot be undone
